title: Zowe MCP CLI Plugin Tools
description: Schema for CLI bridge plugin YAML files (*-tools.yaml). Defines how Zowe CLI plugin commands map to MCP tools via the CLI bridge in packages/zowe-mcp-server/src/tools/cli-bridge/.

plugin.description: Plugin identifier used as a prefix in tool names and log messages (e.g. 'endevor'). Must match the prefix in the companion *-commands.yaml file.

displayName.description: Human-readable display name shown in error dialogs and logs (e.g. 'Endevor'). Falls back to 'plugin' when absent.

activeDescription.description: Default description variant for all tools that do not specify their own activeDescription. Can be overridden at runtime by the ZOWE_MCP_CLI_DESC_VARIANT env var or --cli-plugin-desc-variant flag. Common values: 'cli', 'optimized'.

connectionErrorPatterns.description: JavaScript regex patterns matched against CLI error messages that are always treated as fatal configuration errors (stop: true), overriding retryableErrorPatterns and fatalOnCliError. Use for connection/network/driver-level errors the LLM cannot recover from (e.g. 'SQL30081N' for Db2 network failure, 'SQL1042C' for driver initialisation failure).

retryableErrorPatterns.description: JavaScript regex patterns matched against CLI error messages that produce a retryable tool execution error (isError: true, no stop). Applied only when no connectionErrorPatterns entry matches. Use when an error message reliably indicates the CLI reached the back-end service and the LLM can correct the input and retry (e.g. '\\[IBM\\]\\[CLI Driver\\]\\[DB2\\]' to identify Db2 SQL execution errors that reached the engine).

defaultCliErrorFatal.description: Default error treatment when neither connectionErrorPatterns nor retryableErrorPatterns match, and the tool has no fatalOnCliError set. true (default) = fatal (stop: true). false = retryable. Keeping the default as true preserves backward compatibility for plugins that do not declare any patterns.

profiles.description: Named profile type definitions. Keys are arbitrary type identifiers (e.g. 'connection', 'location'). The key becomes the prefix for the auto-generated list/set tool parameters (e.g. 'connectionId', 'locationId').

tools.description: List of MCP tool definitions. Each entry maps one Zowe CLI command to one MCP tool.

descriptionVariants.description: Per-variant description strings. Any variant name is valid (e.g. 'cli', 'optimized'). Any value may use a '$.dotted.path' JSON reference that is resolved against the companion *-commands.yaml file at load time.

cli.description: Original wording from the Zowe CLI --help text. May be a '$.dotted.path' reference into the companion *-commands.yaml (e.g. '$.endevor.list.environments.description').

optimized.description: LLM-optimized variant generated by the rephrase-tool-descriptions script. Must end with sentence-terminating punctuation (. ! ?).

additionalProperties.description: Custom description variant. Any string key is valid.

profileFieldDef.description: A single field in a named profile type (e.g. host, user, environment). Values in the field are passed as CLI options when invoking Zowe CLI.

name.description: Field name used as the key in named profile instances (camelCase, e.g. 'host', 'user', 'environment', 'stageNumber').

cliOption.description: CLI option name without '--' (e.g. 'host', 'env', 'sn'). When set, the field value is passed as --<cliOption> <value>.

description.description: Plain description string for all variants. May be a '$.dotted.path' JSON reference resolved against the companion *-commands.yaml file.

required.description: When true, this field must be present in every profile of this type.

default.description: Default value injected when the field is absent from the profile and the tool call arguments. Useful for wildcard defaults (e.g. default: '*').

isUsername.description: When true, this field's value is the username used for password lookup (keyed as 'user@host'). Only one field per profile type should have this flag.

profileTypeDef.description: Defines one named-profile type in the profiles: block. Two common archetypes: connection (required: true, perToolOverride: false — set once, used for auth) and location (required: false, perToolOverride: true — fields injected per tool call).

name.description: Human-readable label used in auto-generated list/set tool descriptions and error messages (e.g. 'Endevor connection', 'Endevor location').

toolListName.description: MCP tool name for the auto-generated list-profiles tool (camelCase, e.g. 'endevorListConnections'). Lists all configured profiles of this type.

toolSetName.description: MCP tool name for the auto-generated set-profile tool (camelCase, e.g. 'endevorSetConnection'). Activates a profile or sets the virtual context.

toolAddProfileName.description: Optional MCP tool name for adding a named profile and persisting to zoweMCP.cliPluginConfiguration / --cli-plugin-configuration file (requires server wiring).

toolRemoveProfileName.description: Optional MCP tool name for removing a named profile by id and persisting.

required.description: When true, a profile of this type must be active (or passed as <typeKey>Id) before any tool can be invoked. Auto-selects when exactly one profile is configured.

perToolOverride.description: When true, individual fields of this type are injected into each tool's input schema (controlled per-tool by locationParams). When false, only a <typeKey>Id override parameter is exposed.

listDescription.description: Plain description for the auto-generated list tool. Auto-generated from 'name' when absent.

listDescriptions.description: Per-variant descriptions for the auto-generated list tool.

setDescription.description: Plain description for the auto-generated set tool. Auto-generated from 'name' when absent.

setDescriptions.description: Per-variant descriptions for the auto-generated set tool.

fields.description: Profile fields (non-sensitive; passwords are managed via the password store, not here).

pluginListPaginationConfig.description: Plugin-level defaults for list pagination. Inherited by every tool that uses 'pagination: list' or matches applyToPattern.

defaultLimit.description: Default page size returned when the caller omits 'limit' (default: 200).

maxLimit.description: Hard upper bound on 'limit' values from callers. Values above this are clamped (default: 1000).

maxResults.description: When set and the full CLI result exceeds this count, the tool uses MCP elicitation (when supported) or returns an error asking the user to narrow the query before fetching all results.

cacheTtlSeconds.description: How long (in seconds) to keep cached CLI results before re-invoking (default: 300).

applyToPattern.description: Glob pattern(s) matched against tool names to automatically apply list pagination without a per-tool pagination: block. Supports '*' wildcard. Matching is case-insensitive. Example: '*List*' matches 'endevorListElements'.

pluginContentPaginationConfig.description: Plugin-level defaults for content windowing (line-by-line pagination). Inherited by every tool with outputPath: stdout or that matches applyToPattern.

defaultLineCount.description: Default number of lines returned per window when the caller omits 'lineCount' (default: 1000).

cacheTtlSeconds.description: How long (in seconds) to keep cached CLI results before re-invoking (default: 300).

applyToStdout.description: When true (the default), content pagination is automatically applied to every tool with outputPath: 'stdout'. Set to false to require explicit per-tool 'pagination: content'.

applyToPattern.description: Additional glob pattern(s) matched against tool names to automatically apply content pagination (in addition to the applyToStdout rule). Supports '*' wildcard.

pluginPaginationConfig.description: Plugin-level pagination defaults and auto-application rules. Placed at the root of the plugin YAML next to 'plugin:', 'profiles:', 'tools:'. Tools can inherit these values with 'pagination: list' or 'pagination: content', or be auto-detected based on outputPath and name patterns.

paginationDef.description: Full per-tool pagination definition. Use the 'list' or 'content' shorthand on the tool's pagination: field when plugin-level defaults are sufficient.

type.description: 'list' adds offset/limit parameters and wraps the output array in a ToolResponseEnvelope with ListResultMeta. 'content' adds startLine/lineCount parameters and wraps text output with ReadResultMeta.

defaultLimit.description: (list only) Default page size when the caller omits 'limit'. Overrides plugin-level pagination.list.defaultLimit.

maxLimit.description: (list only) Hard upper bound on 'limit' values from callers. Overrides plugin-level pagination.list.maxLimit.

maxResults.description: (list only) Maximum total results before triggering elicitation or an informational error. Overrides plugin-level pagination.list.maxResults.

defaultLineCount.description: (content only) Default lines per window when the caller omits 'lineCount'. Overrides plugin-level pagination.content.defaultLineCount.

cacheTtlSeconds.description: How long (in seconds) to keep cached CLI results. Overrides plugin-level defaults.

toolPaginationSpec.description: Per-tool pagination configuration. 'list' and 'content' inherit plugin-level defaults. false opts out of auto-detection. An object overrides specific fields from the plugin defaults.

oneOf.description: Inherit plugin-level pagination defaults for the given type ('list' or 'content').
Explicitly opt out of pagination and auto-detection for this tool.
Full or partial pagination definition. Unset fields are filled from plugin-level defaults.

valueMap.description: Optional mapping from friendly MCP parameter values to CLI argument values. When present, the MCP tool accepts the friendly names (the map keys) and translates them to the CLI values before invoking Zowe CLI. Raw CLI values (case-insensitive) are also accepted. Example: { source: ES, history: EH } — both 'source' and 'ES' are accepted; CLI always receives '--option ES'.

additionalProperties.description: CLI value to pass for this friendly name.

pluginParam.description: A single extra parameter for a tool. Each param adds one field to the MCP tool's input schema and maps it to a CLI option or positional argument.

name.description: MCP parameter name exposed in the tool's input schema (camelCase, e.g. 'environment', 'search', 'columnFrom').

cliOption.description: CLI option name without '--' (e.g. 'env', 'sea', 'search-from'). The value is passed as '--<cliOption> <value>'. Mutually exclusive with cliPositional.

cliPositional.description: When true, the value is passed as a positional argument (the first bare word after the sub-command), not as --option value. Mutually exclusive with cliOption.

description.description: Plain description string used as fallback for all variants. May be a '$.dotted.path' JSON reference resolved against the companion *-commands.yaml file (e.g. '$.endevor.list.elements.options.data').

descriptions.description: Per-variant description strings (same resolution order as tool descriptions).

required.description: When true, this parameter must be provided by the caller.

default.description: Default value used when the caller omits this parameter. Applied after location context defaults.

pluginToolDef.description: A single MCP tool definition. Maps one Zowe CLI command to one MCP tool registered on the server.

toolName.description: MCP tool name (camelCase with plugin prefix, e.g. 'endevorListElements'). Appears as 'mcp_zowe_endevorListElements' in VS Code Copilot.

zoweCommand.description: The Zowe CLI command string to execute without 'zowe' prefix or '--rfj' (e.g. 'endevor list elements'). The bridge prepends 'zowe' and appends '--rfj' plus connection profile arguments.

activeDescription.description: Description variant to use for this specific tool. Overrides plugin-level activeDescription and ZOWE_MCP_CLI_DESC_VARIANT. Common values: 'cli', 'optimized'.

descriptions.description: Description variants for this tool. At least one variant should be provided. The first sentence appears in the MCP tools table in docs/mcp-reference.md.

resourceEffectLevel.description: Resource effect level for capability-tier gating. Determines which capability tier is required to register this tool and controls the MCP hints (readOnlyHint, destructiveHint) derived at startup. Accepts a name or a number (0–4). Defaults to 'read' when omitted.

oneOf.description: Named resource effect level: 'none' (0) = no resource effect (server management), 'read' (1) = read resources, 'update' (2) = create/write/modify, 'delete' (3) = delete/cancel, 'execute' (4) = submit jobs / run commands.
Numeric resource effect level: 0 = none, 1 = read, 2 = update, 3 = delete, 4 = execute.

readOnlyHint.description: Deprecated — use resourceEffectLevel instead. Ignored when resourceEffectLevel is set.

destructiveHint.description: Deprecated — use resourceEffectLevel instead. Ignored when resourceEffectLevel is set.

locationParams.description: Controls which fields from perToolOverride: true profile types are injected as optional parameters into this tool's input schema. true = all fields; string[] = named subset; false/absent = none.

oneOf.description: true injects all fields from every perToolOverride: true profile type.
Array of field names to inject (subset of the perToolOverride: true profile type's fields).

params.description: Additional tool-specific parameters beyond the location params. Each param adds one input field and maps it to a CLI option or positional argument.

excludeConnectionFields.description: Connection profile field names to exclude from CLI args for this tool. Use when a command does not accept certain session options (e.g. 'instance' for commands that don't take --instance).

outputPath.description: Key to extract from the parsed --rfj JSON response. Default: 'data'. Use 'stdout' for text output (e.g. print element commands). Use '.' for the entire response body. Supports dot-separated paths (e.g. 'data.items').

fatalOnCliError.description: When false, CLI invocation failures are returned as ordinary tool execution errors (isError: true) rather than fatal configuration errors (stop: true). Use for tools where the LLM may send invalid input that it can correct and retry (e.g. db2ExecuteSql where a bad SQL statement should be retryable). Defaults to true.
